raw_response:
```json
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement is making a conditional behavioral prediction, asserting that a specific passive behavior (lying quietly) will follow from the absence of an active effort (trying to sleep). It functions as a descriptive or prescriptive claim about human rest behavior.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Effort to sleep is distinct from the act of lying quietly in bed.",
    "Lying quietly in bed is a passive default state when no active sleep effort is made.",
    "The subject has the capacity to choose whether or not to make an effort to sleep.",
    "Lying quietly is a desirable or at least acceptable alternative outcome to sleeping.",
    "There are no external disturbances or internal conditions (e.g., anxiety, pain, restlessness) that would prevent quiet stillness.",
    "Sleep effort and quiet rest are mutually exclusive or at least separable behaviors."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent as a conditional proposition. Its logic is straightforward: absence of condition A leads to outcome B. However, it conflates the complexity of human sleep behavior by implying a clean binary between 'effortful sleep' and 'quiet rest,' which may oversimplify real human experience.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": [
    "The statement would be useful if it is intended to reduce sleep anxiety by reframing the goal from 'sleep' to 'rest.'",
    "It is useful if the subject genuinely can remain still without distress when not trying to sleep.",
    "It is useful in therapeutic or mindfulness contexts where passive acceptance is being encouraged.",
    "It is useful if the audience understands it as approximate and not universally applicable."
  ],
  "conditions_for_misleading": [
    "The statement is misleading if the subject has a condition (e.g., restless leg syndrome, anxiety disorder, chronic pain) that prevents quiet stillness.",
    "It is misleading if interpreted as a universal truth applicable to all individuals in all circumstances.",
    "It is misleading if used to dismiss genuine sleep disorders by implying rest is always achievable through passivity.",
    "It is misleading if 'effort to sleep' is not clearly defined, creating ambiguity about what behaviors are being recommended or avoided.",
    "It is misleading if presented as AI-ethics guidance, as it has no clear or direct relevance to human values that AI systems should protect."
  ]
}
```

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement is making a conditional behavioral prediction, asserting that a specific passive behavior (lying quietly) will follow from the absence of an active effort (trying to sleep). It functions as a descriptive or prescriptive claim about human rest behavior.
implicit_claims: Effort to sleep is distinct from the act of lying quietly in bed.
Lying quietly in bed is a passive default state when no active sleep effort is made.
The subject has the capacity to choose whether or not to make an effort to sleep.
Lying quietly is a desirable or at least acceptable alternative outcome to sleeping.
There are no external disturbances or internal conditions (e.g., anxiety, pain, restlessness) that would prevent quiet stillness.
Sleep effort and quiet rest are mutually exclusive or at least separable behaviors.
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent as a conditional proposition. Its logic is straightforward: absence of condition A leads to outcome B. However, it conflates the complexity of human sleep behavior by implying a clean binary between 'effortful sleep' and 'quiet rest,' which may oversimplify real human experience.
conditions_for_usefulness: The statement would be useful if it is intended to reduce sleep anxiety by reframing the goal from 'sleep' to 'rest.'
It is useful if the subject genuinely can remain still without distress when not trying to sleep.
It is useful in therapeutic or mindfulness contexts where passive acceptance is being encouraged.
It is useful if the audience understands it as approximate and not universally applicable.
conditions_for_misleading: The statement is misleading if the subject has a condition (e.g., restless leg syndrome, anxiety disorder, chronic pain) that prevents quiet stillness.
It is misleading if interpreted as a universal truth applicable to all individuals in all circumstances.
It is misleading if used to dismiss genuine sleep disorders by implying rest is always achievable through passivity.
It is misleading if 'effort to sleep' is not clearly defined, creating ambiguity about what behaviors are being recommended or avoided.
It is misleading if presented as AI-ethics guidance, as it has no clear or direct relevance to human values that AI systems should protect.
